summary: A charged capacitor stores energy in its electric field: W = ½·C·U². Because of the square, doubling the voltage quadruples the stored energy.
method: Identify the capacitor's capacitance C and the voltage U across it.
Decide which quantity is unknown — energy, capacitance, or voltage.
Rearrange W = ½·C·U²; solving for U involves a square root.
Use SI units (farads, volts) so the energy comes out in joules.

example.title: Energy in a camera-flash capacitor
steps: The unknown is W, so use W = ½·C·U².
